alignments.pub_claim_text: The optimal stochastic CA maintains critical behavior despite systematic perturbations of its probabilities.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning classification model (FORM) can correctly predict the 10-year hip fracture risk with a validation AUC of 81.44% ± 3.11% for X-rays and 81.04% ± 5.54% for CT projections.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides the same AUC point estimates and adds uncertainty intervals, making the preprint result more precise (clarified) without changing scope or overall confidence level.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FORM significantly outperforms previous methods like the Cox Proportional-Hazards Model and frax in predicting hip fracture risk.
alignments.pub_claim_text: FORM significantly outperforms previous methods like the Cox Proportional-Hazards Model, which had validation AUCs of 70.19 ± 6.58 and 74.72 ± 7.21 on the X-ray cohort.
alignments.rationale: The publication documents outperforming the Cox model with specific numbers but does not explicitly mention comparison to frax; this narrows the original comparative claim to the methods explicitly reported.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FORM outperforms hip aBMD based predictions on both X-ray and CT cohorts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: FORM outperforms hip aBMD based predictions with validation AUCs of 82.67% ± 0.21% compared to 71.82% ± 0.50% for X-rays and 78.41% ± 0.33% compared to 76.55% ± 0.89% for CTs.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides the explicit AUC comparisons supporting the preprint claim, thus clarifying and quantifying the originally stated result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FORM can contribute to improving osteoporosis diagnosis at an early stage.
alignments.pub_claim_text: FORM can contribute to improving osteoporosis diagnosis at an early stage.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed QueryProp framework improves video object detection in terms of both accuracy and speed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed QueryProp framework improves video object detection in terms of both accuracy and speed.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same high-level conclusion with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Query propagation from sparse key frames to dense non-key frames reduces redundant computation on non-key frames.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Query propagation from sparse key frames to dense non-key frames reduces redundant computation on non-key frames.
alignments.rationale: The result is carried over verbatim from preprint to publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Query propagation from previous key frames to the current key frame enhances feature representation through temporal context modeling.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Query propagation from previous key frames to the current key frame enhances feature representation through temporal context modeling.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this mechanism/result without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: QueryProp achieves comparable accuracy with state-of-the-art methods while maintaining a decent accuracy/speed trade-off.
alignments.pub_claim_text: QueryProp achieves comparable accuracy with state-of-the-art methods while maintaining a decent accuracy/speed trade-off.
alignments.rationale: The empirical conclusion and framing are unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AdaptCL is a novel adaptive continual learning method designed to tackle heterogeneity in sequential datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AdaptCL consistently exhibits robust performance across various scenarios, demonstrating its flexibility and general applicability in handling heterogeneous datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes demonstrated robustness and applicability (an empirical instantiation of the preprint's design claim), which clarifies the original general claim though with somewhat different wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AdaptCL employs fine-grained data-driven pruning to adapt to variations in data complexity and dataset size.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AdaptCL employs fine-grained data-driven pruning to adapt to variations in data complexity and dataset size.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is described the same way in both preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AdaptCL utilizes task-agnostic parameter isolation to mitigate the impact of varying degrees of catastrophic forgetting caused by differences in data similarity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AdaptCL utilizes task-agnostic parameter isolation to mitigate the impact of varying degrees of catastrophic forgetting caused by differences in data similarity.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this methodological claim with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AdaptCL consistently exhibits robust performance across various datasets, demonstrating its flexibility and general applicability.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AdaptCL consistently exhibits robust performance across various scenarios, demonstrating its flexibility and general applicability in handling heterogeneous datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication substantiates and restates the claimed robust performance across settings with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed algorithm automatically detects gradual drifts in real-life processes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed algorithm automatically detects gradual drifts in processes where cases of two models overlap over time.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies a particular condition (overlap of two models) under which gradual drifts are detected, narrowing and thereby revising the broader preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithm performs a fully automatic classification of changes into sudden or gradual.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The algorithm performs a fully automatic classification of changes into sudden or gradual types.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same claim about automatic classification with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach has been validated with a synthetic dataset, achieving better detection and classification accuracy compared to state-of-the-art algorithms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach has been validated with a synthetic dataset, achieving better detection and classification accuracy compared to state-of-the-art algorithms.
alignments.rationale: Validation and comparative performance claims are preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Most existing algorithms focus on the detection of sudden changes and overlook other types of changes.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include or restate this background/generalization about other algorithms, indicating the claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method demonstrates a clear improvement in speckle reduction when additional SAR images are included.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Quantitative analysis on datasets with simulated speckle indicates a clear improvement in speckle reduction when additional SAR images are included.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same improvement claim but ties it to quantitative simulated-data analysis and reports at a medium certainty level, which clarifies the evidence and slightly tempers the stated confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The extension of the self-supervised training strategy MERLIN to multi-temporal filtering effectively circumvents the limitations of ground-truth images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The extension of the MERLIN self-supervised training strategy to multitemporal filtering improves speckle reduction in SAR images.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the extension as an empirical improvement (rather than the preprint's framing about circumventing ground-truth limitations) and states the result with higher certainty, thereby clarifying and increasing expressed confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method outperforms competing multi-temporal speckle filtering approaches when applied to stacks of TerraSAR-X images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method outperforms competing multitemporal speckle filtering approaches when applied to stacks of TerraSAR-X images.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the performance claim on TerraSAR-X stacks with equivalent wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed PF-ViT model effectively separates and recognizes disturbance-agnostic emotions from static facial images without the need for paired images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed PF-ViT model effectively separates and recognizes disturbance-agnostic emotions from static facial images.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same core result (separation and recognition of disturbance-agnostic emotions) with effectively identical meaning; minor omission of the 'paired images' phrase does not change the central claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PF-ViT utilizes vanilla Vision Transformers pre-trained as Masked Autoencoders on a large facial expression dataset without emotion labels, resulting in excellent representations.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not mention MAE pretraining or the specific pretraining scheme; this specific methodological claim appears absent from the published claim list.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The auxiliary task of poker face generation during training promotes the disentanglement between emotional and emotion-irrelevant components in the FER model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The auxiliary task of poker face generation during training promotes the disentanglement between emotional and emotion-irrelevant components.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion about poker-face generation promoting disentanglement with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Quantitative and qualitative results demonstrate that PF-ViT surpasses state-of-the-art methods on four popular FER datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Quantitative and qualitative results demonstrate that PF-ViT surpasses state-of-the-art methods on four popular facial expression recognition datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication claim matches the preprint's performance claim verbatim aside from minor wording (FER spelled out).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Graph-SSTV (GSSTV) model effectively addresses the limitations of the spatio-spectral total variation (SSTV) model in removing noise while preserving complex spatial structures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed graph-SSTV (GSSTV) model effectively addresses the limitations of the spatio-spectral total variation (SSTV) model in removing noise while preserving complex spatial structures.
alignments.rationale: The publication replicates the same conclusion about GSSTV addressing SSTV limitations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GSSTV incorporates a weighted spatial difference operator designed based on a graph that reflects the spatial structure of the target hyperspectral image.
alignments.pub_claim_text: GSSTV incorporates a weighted spatial difference operator that is designed based on a graph reflecting the spatial structure of the target hyperspectral image.
alignments.rationale: The contribution description is essentially identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mixed noise removal problem is formulated as a convex optimization problem involving GSSTV, which can be efficiently solved using a primal-dual splitting method.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The mixed noise removal problem is formulated as a convex optimization problem involving GSSTV, which is solved using an efficient algorithm based on the primal-dual splitting method.
alignments.rationale: Both describe the same formulation and solution method with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of GSSTV compared to existing hyperspectral image regularization models for mixed noise removal.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of GSSTV compared to existing hyperspectral image regularization models for mixed noise removal.
alignments.rationale: Experimental effectiveness is stated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed context-aware controller inference approach stabilizes high-dimensional dynamical systems using only the unstable dynamics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed context-aware controller inference approach stabilizes high-dimensional dynamical systems using only the unstable dynamics.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint's stabilization claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Context-aware controller inference learns stabilizing controllers from orders of magnitude fewer data samples than traditional data-driven control techniques and variants of reinforcement learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Context-aware controller inference learns stabilizing controllers from orders of magnitude fewer data samples than traditional data-driven control techniques and variants of reinforcement learning.
alignments.rationale: The data-efficiency result is retained exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The low data requirements of context-aware controller inference are particularly advantageous in data-scarce engineering problems with complex physics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The low data requirements of context-aware controller inference are particularly advantageous in data-scarce engineering problems with complex physics.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps this conclusion about advantage in data-scarce problems unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Pseudonymisation by face-blurring video recordings is a viable approach for sharing movement video data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Pseudonymization by face-blurring video recordings is a viable approach for analyzing infant neuromotor functions.
alignments.rationale: The publication narrows and clarifies the application focus from general sharing of movement video data to the specific task of analyzing infant neuromotor function.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The video redaction did not affect classification accuracy for either human assessors or computer vision methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The redaction did not affect classification accuracy for either human assessors or artificial intelligence methods.
alignments.rationale: The claim that redaction did not affect classification accuracy is preserved with equivalent meaning; wording differs only slightly (computer vision -> AI).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There are currently no shared expert-annotated large data repositories for infant movement analyses.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not restate the specific assertion about the absence of shared expert-annotated large repositories, so this preprint claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Efficient and privacy rule-conforming approaches for deidentifying video data should be explored in scientific and clinical fields beyond movement assessments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Efforts at data de-identification can maintain data utility while allowing for the sharing of behavioral video data.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the recommendation as a broader conclusion that de-identification can preserve utility and enable sharing of behavioral video data, expanding the original recommendation beyond the specific fields mentioned.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The work encourages more innovative solutions to share and merge stand-alone video datasets into large data pools.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit call to merge datasets into larger pools; this specific emphasis on merging/ pooling datasets was not a distinct preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Integrated photonic neural networks (IPNNs) offer substantial improvements in computing speed and energy efficiency compared to conventional electronic AI accelerators.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Integrated photonic neural networks (IPNNs) offer substantial improvements in computing speed and energy efficiency compared to conventional electronic AI accelerators.
alignments.rationale: The high-level conclusion about IPNN benefits is preserved identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The impact of uncertainties and imprecisions in IPNNs can vary widely based on the tuned parameters, physical location, and nature of the imperfections.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The impact of uncertainties and imprecisions in IPNNs can vary widely based on the tuned parameters, physical location, and nature of the imperfections.
alignments.rationale: The textual claim is the same, but the publication labels the certainty/result as lower (medium) versus the preprint (high), so confidence is marked as tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Under multiple simultaneous imperfections, the IPNN inferencing accuracy can degrade by up to 46%, even with imperfection parameters restricted within a small range.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Under multiple simultaneous imperfections, the IPNN inferencing accuracy can degrade by up to 46.
alignments.rationale: The magnitude and direction of the degradation result is preserved (minor formatting difference only).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The inferencing accuracy of IPNNs is sensitive to imperfections affecting the MZIs in the linear layers next to the input layer.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The inferencing accuracy of IPNNs is sensitive to imperfections affecting the MZIs in the linear layers next to the input layer.
alignments.rationale: The sensitivity-to-imperfections claim is carried over without substantive change in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Smart grids are vulnerable to stealthy false data injection attacks (SFDIAs) that can bypass residual-based bad data detection mechanisms.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate or address the general vulnerability claim about smart grids or bypassing residual-based detectors; it focuses on the proposed PowerFDNet contributions instead.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Methods based on deep learning technology have shown promising accuracy in the detection of SFDIAs.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include the general background claim about deep learning methods being promising; it only presents the specific PowerFDNet results.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed spatiotemporal deep network, PowerFDNet, can effectively model the spatiotemporal structure of measurements for SFDIA detection.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed PowerFDNet can effectively model the spatiotemporal structure of measurements for SFDIA detection.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this claim verbatim about PowerFDNet's ability to model spatiotemporal structure for SFDIA detection.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PowerFDNet achieved significant improvement compared with the state-of-the-art SFDIA detection methods in case studies on benchmark smart grids.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PowerFDNet achieved significant improvement compared with the state-of-the-art SFDIA detection methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same result claim of significant improvement relative to state-of-the-art methods, matching the preprint's result statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An IoT-oriented lightweight prototype of PowerFDNet has been implemented and tested for mobile devices, demonstrating potential applications on mobile devices.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An IoT-oriented lightweight prototype of PowerFDNet has been implemented and tested for mobile devices.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes the same implementation/testing claim for an IoT/mobile prototype; the wording is slightly tighter but conveys the same contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed NFDLM framework demonstrates superior performance in detecting DDoS attacks compared to Long Short Term Memory (LSTM) and simple Artificial Neural Networks (ANN).
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed NFDLM framework demonstrates superior results in DDoS attack detection compared to Long Short Term Memory (LSTM) and simple ANN models.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same comparative performance claim against LSTM and simple ANN models with equivalent emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The detection performance of the NFDLM framework achieves approximately 99% accuracy for detecting attacks from botnets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The detection performance of the NFDLM framework achieves approximately 99% accuracy for detecting attacks from botnets.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same specific performance figure (≈99% accuracy) for botnet attack detection.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Four different models were designed and compared, including two based on ANN and two based on LSTM, to detect DDoS attack types.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study compares four different models for DDoS attack detection, including two based on ANN and two based on LSTM.
alignments.rationale: The publication carries forward the same methodological claim about designing and comparing four models (two ANN, two LSTM).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The mathematical conditions for a Machine Learning classifier to exhibit error probabilities that vanish exponentially are provided.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We provide the mathematical conditions for a ML classifier to exhibit error probabilities that vanish exponentially.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same core theoretical claim about conditions for exponential vanishing of error probabilities.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The error rate $I$ depends on the given training set, which is assumed to be of finite size.
alignments.rationale: No explicit published claim restates the specific assertion about the dependence of a named error rate I on a finite training set; the publication focuses on verifiability and approximations instead.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classification error probability convergence to zero and its rate can be computed on a portion of the dataset available for training.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We compute an accurate analytical approximation of the classification performance for both small and large values of n.
alignments.rationale: The publication replaces the more specific 'compute on a portion of the dataset' statement with a stronger, broader claim of accurate analytical approximations across small and large n, expanding applicability and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The normalized D3F statistic converges to a Gaussian distribution for large enough $n$.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We establish the convergence of the normalized D3F statistic to a Gaussian distribution.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same convergence result and states it without the earlier 'for large enough n' qualification, and with higher reported certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Approximate error probability curves are provided, which represent the most representative sub-exponential terms of the error probabilities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We compute an accurate analytical approximation of the classification performance for both small and large values of n.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents a clarified and stronger formulation of the approximation claim (accurate analytical approximations for varying n) compared to the preprint's description of approximate sub-exponential curves.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed two-stage matching mechanism controls the similarity between target and reference images at the object-/attribute-level.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed two-stage matching mechanism controls the similarity between target and reference images at the object-/attribute-level.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim about the two-stage matching mechanism controlling object-/attribute-level similarity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TransDIC, the developed Transformer-based Ref-DIC baseline, extracts visual features from the target image and encodes differences between objects in the target and reference images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: TransDIC, the developed Transformer-based Ref-DIC baseline, can generate distinctive captions by extracting visual features and encoding differences between objects in target and reference images.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same description of TransDIC's architecture/processing and ties it directly to the capability to generate distinctive captions; the core technical claim is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate that TransDIC can generate distinctive captions and outperforms several state-of-the-art models on the two new benchmarks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: TransDIC outperforms several state-of-the-art models on the two new benchmarks across different metrics.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the experimental finding that TransDIC outperforms prior models on the new benchmarks, consistent with the preprint's result claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new evaluation metric named DisCIDEr has been proposed for Ref-DIC, which evaluates both the accuracy and distinctiveness of the generated captions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new evaluation metric named DisCIDEr has been proposed for Ref-DIC, which evaluates both the accuracy and distinctiveness of generated captions.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes the identical contribution claim about proposing DisCIDEr and its evaluation focus.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TRUST-LAPSE achieves state-of-the-art results with AUROCs of 84.1 (vision), 73.9 (audio), and 77.1 (clinical EEGs), outperforming baselines by over 10 points.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The TRUST-LAPSE framework achieves state-of-the-art results with AUROCs of 84.1 for vision, 73.9 for audio, and 77.1 for clinical EEGs, outperforming baselines by over 10 points.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the exact quantitative performance claims and comparative statement from the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our sequential mistrust scores achieve high drift detection rates; over 90% of the streams show < 20% error for all domains.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The sequential mistrust scores achieve high drift detection rates, with over 90 of the streams showing less than 20% error across all domains.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the sequential mistrust score performance claim with effectively the same statistics and meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We expose critical failures in popular baselines that remain insensitive to input semantic content, rendering them unfit for real-world model monitoring.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed mistrust scoring framework exposes critical failures in popular baselines that are insensitive to input semantic content, making them unfit for real-world model monitoring.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the preprint's conclusion about baseline failures due to insensitivity to semantic content and their unsuitability for monitoring.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our mistrust scores are more robust and provide explainability for easy adoption into practice.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The latent-space mistrust scores provide explainability and robustness, facilitating their easy adoption into practice.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same claims of robustness and explainability supporting adoption; wording is slightly different but the meaning is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Developing building occupant personas is proven to be an effective method for human-centered smart building design.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Developing building occupant personas is proven to be an effective method for human-centered smart building design.
alignments.rationale: The two statements are effectively identical in wording and meaning, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed machine learning-based semi-automated approach can generate building occupant personas.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study proposes a machine learning-based approach for occupant characteristics classification and prediction.
alignments.rationale: The preprint emphasizes semi-automated persona generation while the publication reframes the contribution as classification/prediction of occupant characteristics, changing the claim focus (narrowing from persona generation to characteristics prediction).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models achieve an average accuracy of 61% and accuracy over 90% for certain occupant characteristics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models achieved moderate accuracy in predicting most of the occupant characteristics and significantly higher accuracy (over 90%) for attributes including the number of occupants in the household, their age group, and preferred usage of primary cooling equipment.
alignments.rationale: The publication gives more contextual detail (labels the ~61% as moderate and specifies which attributes exceed 90%), clarifying rather than changing the core result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results of the study show the feasibility of using machine learning techniques for the development of building occupant personas.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results of the study show the feasibility of using machine learning techniques for occupant characteristics prediction.
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts the emphasis from persona development to characteristics prediction and reports lower certainty (high→medium), indicating a narrowed and somewhat tempered conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The GAN-CEST 3D acquisition time was 42-52 seconds, which is 70% shorter than CEST-MRF.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The GAN-ST 3D acquisition time was 42-52 seconds, which is 70% shorter than CEST-MRF.
alignments.rationale: The numeric result is the same but the model name changed (GAN-CEST → GAN-ST); meaning is preserved so this is a clarification/renaming rather than substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The quantitative reconstruction of the entire brain took 0.8 seconds.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The quantitative reconstruction of the entire brain took 0.8 seconds.
alignments.rationale: Identical quantitative reconstruction time reported in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An excellent agreement was observed between the ground truth and GAN-based L-arginine concentration and pH values with Pearson's r > 0.97 and NRMSE < 1.5%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An excellent agreement was observed between the ground truth and GAN-based L-arginine concentration and pH values, with Pearson's r > 0.95 and ICC > 0.88.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports different/less stringent summary statistics (r threshold relaxed, NRMSE removed, ICC added), modifying the quantitative characterization of agreement and thereby tempering the original numerical claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GAN-CEST has demonstrated improved performance and reduced noise compared to MRF in regions with large susceptibility artifacts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: GAN-ST has demonstrated improved performance and reduced noise compared to MRF in regions with large susceptibility artifacts.
alignments.rationale: The core claim about improved performance/noise is preserved (with a model name change), while the publication raises certainty from medium to high, reflecting amplification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: GAN-CEST can substantially reduce the acquisition time for quantitative semisolid MT/CEST mapping while retaining performance even when facing pathologies and scanner models that were not available during training.
alignments.pub_claim_text: GAN-ST can substantially reduce the acquisition time for quantitative semi-solid MT/CEST mapping while retaining performance even when facing pathologies and scanner models that were not available during training.
alignments.rationale: The substantive claim about reduced acquisition time and robustness is preserved; the publication expresses higher confidence (medium→high) and renames the method.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Noise Aware-LOCCNet (NA-LOCCNet) demonstrates significant advantages over existing LOCC protocols designed for noiseless communications.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach, Noise Aware-LOCCNet (NA-LOCCNet), demonstrates significant advantages over existing protocols designed for noiseless communications.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim about NA-LOCCNet's advantages with effectively identical wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study addresses the design of LOCC protocols in the context of noisy classical communication channels.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study addresses the design of LOCC protocols in the context of noisy classical communication channels.
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Quantum machine learning tools are utilized to optimize local processing through parameterized quantum circuits (PQCs) for tasks such as quantum entanglement distillation and quantum state discrimination.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Quantum machine learning tools are utilized to optimize local processing through parameterized quantum circuits (PQCs) for quantum entanglement distillation and quantum state discrimination.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same description of the use of PQCs and quantum machine learning tools.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduced AI-based framework demonstrates improved deep, super-resolution, wide-field radio-interferometric imaging.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AI-based framework for deep, super-resolution, wide-field radio interferometric imaging demonstrates improved image formation capabilities.
alignments.rationale: Both versions claim improved imaging capabilities; wording differs slightly but the substantive claim is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework validates image formation from 19 gigabytes of MeerKAT data at 1053 and 1399 MHz.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework successfully reconstructs images from 19 GB of MeerKAT data at 1053 and 1399 MHz.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result about reconstructing images from the specified MeerKAT data.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The recovered maps exhibit significantly more resolution and dynamic range than the traditional CLEAN method.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The recovered maps exhibit significantly more resolution and dynamic range than the traditional CLEAN method.
alignments.rationale: Identical comparative result against the CLEAN method in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The algorithmic framework utilizes a 'plug-and-play' scheme with a denoising operator for image regularization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed parallel algorithm implementation enables scalability to large data and image dimensions.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes a parallel implementation and scalability, shifting emphasis away from the plug-and-play denoiser; this represents a substantive change in the described algorithmic contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Most segmenters focus on pixel classification, and polygonization of this output has not been used as a target for the latest competition on historical document.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Most segmenters focus on pixel classification, and polygonization of this output has not been used as a target for the latest competition on historical document analysis.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same observation; wording is essentially identical.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Shifting the task from a pixel classification-based polygonization to an object detection using isothetic rectangles is proposed for efficiency.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Shifting the task from pixel classification-based polygonization to object detection using isothetic rectangles is proposed for efficiency.
alignments.rationale: The proposed methodological shift is stated the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: YOLOv5 severely outperforms Kraken on small datasets (1110 samples and below) in terms of segmentation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: YOLOv5 severely outperforms Kraken on small datasets (1110 samples and below) in terms of segmentation.
alignments.rationale: Result is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two datasets for training and evaluation on historical documents have been released along with a new package, YALTAi.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two datasets for training and evaluation on historical documents have been released along with a new package, YALTAi.
alignments.rationale: The release of datasets and the package is reported unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Adaptive Soft Contrastive Learning (ASCL) transforms the original instance discrimination task into a multi-instance soft discrimination task. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Adaptive Soft Contrastive Learning (ASCL) transforms the original instance discrimination task into a multi-instance soft discrimination task. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint wording and meaning exactly, so the claim is unchanged in content, scope and confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The GNN structure called GAT-MLP provides a new unified network framework for multimodal learning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The convolutional neural network (CNN) achieves a relative Root Mean Squared Error of 2% to 3% for the temperature field.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The convolutional neural network (CNN) achieves a relative Root Mean Squared Error of less than 5% for the temperature field in the solidifying region.
alignments.rationale: The publication relaxes the numeric precision (to <5%) and adds the qualifier "in the solidifying region," thus revising and narrowing the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CNN achieves an average Intersection over Union score of 80% to 90% in predicting the melt pool area.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CNN achieves an average Intersection over Union score of 80% to 90% in predicting the three-dimensional geometry of the melt pool.
alignments.rationale: The IoU numbers are preserved but the target is changed from 2D area to 3D geometry, which revises and broadens the scope of the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The thermal field can be instantly obtained for any arbitrary time step without the need to iterate and compute all the steps.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The temperature field can be obtained in a matter of a few seconds for any arbitrary time step using the trained CNN.
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies implementation (using the trained CNN) and replaces "instantly" with "a few seconds," which tempers the confidence in immediacy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Convolutional neural networks provide poor explanations for classification decisions in the case of multivariate data series.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Convolutional neural networks provide poor explanations for classification decisions in the case of multivariate data series.
alignments.rationale: The limitation is repeated verbatim in the publication with the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method, dCAM, highlights both the temporal and dimensional discriminant information for multivariate time series classification.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method, dCAM, highlights both the temporal and dimensional discriminant information for multivariate time series.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is preserved in the publication with equivalent wording and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate that dCAM is more accurate than previous approaches for discriminant feature discovery and classification explanation in multivariate time series.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate that dCAM is more accurate than previous approaches for discriminant feature discovery and classification explanation in multivariate time series.
alignments.rationale: The experimental result is carried forward unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed convolutional architecture enables the comparison of dimensions in multivariate time series data.
alignments.rationale: The specific claim about enabling comparison of dimensions is not present in the publication; instead the publication adds a stronger claim, so this preprint claim is removed.

alignments.pub_claim_text: dCAM is the only viable solution for discriminant feature discovery and classification explanation in multivariate time series.
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces a stronger, exclusive claim about dCAM being the only viable solution that did not appear in the preprint; this is treated as an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using RAFT as a baseline, we propose a novel multi-scale neural network that combines several hierarchical concepts within a single estimation framework.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using RAFT as a baseline, we propose a novel multi-scale neural network that combines several hierarchical concepts within a single estimation framework.
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments on MPI Sintel and KITTI clearly demonstrate the benefits of our approach.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments on MPI Sintel and KITTI clearly demonstrate the benefits of our approach.
alignments.rationale: The experimental claim is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach shows substantial improvements compared to RAFT.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach shows substantial improvements compared to RAFT.
alignments.rationale: The comparative improvement claim remains the same in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method achieves state-of-the-art results, particularly in non-occluded regions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method achieves state-of-the-art results, particularly in non-occluded regions.
alignments.rationale: The SOTA claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Model-based Reinforcement Learning and Control can effectively address sequential decision making problems in robotics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Model-based reinforcement learning and control can effectively address sequential decision making problems in robotics.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about model-based RL effectiveness is restated unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Real-world robotics systems face challenges such as irregular observations and dramatic changes in environment dynamics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Irregular/asynchronous observations and actions, along with dramatic changes in environment dynamics, limit the applicability of existing methods in real-world robotics systems.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the limitation with more precise language (including actions and applicability), clarifying the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework utilizes meta-learning to create adaptive dynamics models for continuous-time prediction and control.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework utilizes meta-learning to create adaptive dynamics models for continuous-time prediction and control.
alignments.rationale: The contribution description is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach is task-agnostic and can be easily adapted to new tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach is task-agnostic and can be easily adapted to new tasks.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about task-agnostic adaptability is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Evaluations of the proposed method were conducted in both robot simulations and on a real industrial robot.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Evaluations of the proposed method were conducted in two different robot simulations and on a real industrial robot.
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies the evaluation setup by specifying two different simulations, which refines the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed hybrid model and learning-based adaptive navigation filter improves position error by 25%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed hybrid model and learning-based adaptive navigation filter improves position error by 25%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Text and quantitative claim are identical between preprint and publication, so meaning, scope, and confidence remain unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The process noise covariance is subject to change due to vehicle dynamics and sensor measurement variations. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The process noise covariance in navigation systems is subject to change due to vehicle dynamics and sensor measurement variations. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same observation with near-identical wording; no substantive change in meaning, scope, or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed hybrid learning method can be applied to any navigation filter and relevant estimation problem. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed hybrid learning method can be applied to any navigation filter and relevant estimation problem. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is carried forward verbatim, preserving meaning, scope, and expressed confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose a reinforcement learning algorithm for chemical process design based on a state-of-the-art actor-critic logic. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A reinforcement learning algorithm for chemical process design based on actor‐critic logic is proposed. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Core contribution is the same; wording is slightly condensed but the claim content and confidence are preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrate the potential of our method to design economically viable flowsheets in an illustrative case study comprising equilibrium reactions, azeotropic separation, and recycles. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method demonstrates the potential to design economically viable flowsheets in an illustrative case study. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication keeps the main result but omits the detailed case-study description (equilibrium reactions, azeotropes, recycles), thus slightly narrowing/summarizing the scope while preserving the result and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results show quick learning in discrete, continuous, and hybrid action spaces. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results indicate quick learning in discrete, continuous, and hybrid action spaces. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim content is the same, but the publication lowers the expressed certainty from high to medium, so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The flexible architecture of the proposed reinforcement learning agent is predestined to include large action-state spaces and an interface to process simulators in future research. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method is predestined to include large action‐state spaces and an interface to process simulators in future research. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The future-work conclusion remains the same but the publication expresses lower certainty (medium→low), so the claim is tempered rather than changed in scope.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed algorithm represents chemical processes as graphs and utilizes graph convolutional neural networks to learn from these graphs. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This methodological detail (graph representation and GCN use) is present in the publication but absent from the preprint, so it is an added claim in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach to train Wasserstein Conditional Generative Adversarial Neural Networks (W-CGANs) enhances scalability by using multiple generators trained concurrently on a single data label. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach to train Wasserstein conditional generative adversarial neural networks (W-CGANs) enhances scalability by using multiple generators trained concurrently on a single data label. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Claim is carried forward verbatim in meaning and scope, so unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of the Wasserstein metric in the training of each generator reduces the risk of cycling and stabilizes the training process. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of the Wasserstein metric in the training of each generator reduces the risk of cycling and stabilizes the training process. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Identical conclusion across versions; no substantive change detected.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An improvement in inception score and Frechet inception distance is demonstrated compared to previous results obtained with deep convolutional conditional generative adversarial neural networks (DC-CGANs). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An improvement in inception score and Fréchet inception distance is demonstrated compared to previous results from parallel approaches on deep convolutional conditional generative adversarial neural networks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication slightly clarifies the comparative baseline (phrasing about parallel approaches) but preserves the result and metrics, so the claim is clarified rather than revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Weak scaling is achieved on both datasets using up to 2,000 NVIDIA V100 GPUs on the OLCF supercomputer Summit. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Weak scaling is achieved on both datasets using up to 2000 NVIDIA V100 GPUs on the OLCF supercomputer Summit. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Numeric formatting differs only (comma vs no comma); the scaling claim is identical in meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach learns state machine models to model the runtime behaviour of a cloud environment running multiple microservice applications. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach learns state machine models to model the runtime behaviour of a cloud environment running multiple microservice applications. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is reproduced verbatim; no change in meaning, scope, or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This is the first work that applies state machine models to microservice architectures. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This is the first work that applies state machine models to microservice architectures. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The novelty claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The state machine model can detect different types of attacks launched on the cloud environment. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The state machine model can detect different types of attacks launched on the cloud environment. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Result statement is identical and preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach achieves a balanced accuracy of 99.2% and an F1 score of 0.982 in detecting attacks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach achieves a balanced accuracy of 99.2% and an F1 score of 0.982 in detecting attacks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Performance metrics are unchanged and reported identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method develops a scalable and efficient topology inference for gene regulatory networks using Bayesian optimization and kernel-based methods. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method develops a scalable and efficient topology inference for gene regulatory networks using Bayesian optimization and kernel-based methods. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Contribution is carried forward verbatim; no substantive changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method constructs a Gaussian Process with a topology-inspired kernel function to account for correlation in the likelihood function. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method constructs a Gaussian Process with a topology-inspired kernel function to account for correlation in the likelihood function. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Methodological claim is identical between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Bayesian optimization efficiently searches for the topology with the highest likelihood value by optimally balancing between exploration and exploitation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Bayesian optimization efficiently searches for the topology with the highest likelihood value by optimally balancing between exploration and exploitation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Result description remains the same in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of the proposed method is demonstrated through comprehensive numerical experiments using a well-known mammalian cell-cycle network. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the proposed method is demonstrated through comprehensive numerical experiments using a well-known mammalian cell-cycle network. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Empirical evaluation claim is preserved without substantive changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Bundle MCR framework effectively alleviates issues of interaction sparsity and large output space in bundle recommender systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Bundle MCR extends multi-round conversational recommendation to address interaction sparsity and large output space in bundle recommendation systems.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution and problem addressed (interaction sparsity and large output space) with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Bundle Bert (Bunt) model architecture is capable of recommending items, posting questions, and managing conversations based on bundle-aware conversation states.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Bundle Bert (Bunt) model architecture effectively recommends items, posts questions, and manages conversations based on bundle-aware conversation states.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same functional description of Bunt with no substantive change in claim, scope, or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The two-stage training strategy for Bunt, which includes offline pre-training and online fine-tuning, enhances the model's performance through user interactions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The two-stage training strategy for Bunt, involving offline pre-training and online fine-tuning, enhances the model's performance through user interactions.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same two-stage training claim and its asserted effect on performance via interactions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments on multiple offline datasets and human evaluations demonstrate the effectiveness of extending MCR frameworks to bundle settings.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not mention experiments on multiple offline datasets or human evaluations; this experimental-claim is not represented in the publication claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Bundle MCR encodes user feedback as conversation states and manages user interactions across multiple items and related tags.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes an explicit claim about encoding user feedback and managing interactions across items/tags that was not present among the preprint claims as listed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed hybrid neural network methodology can accurately predict at least one period of ship roll motion in high sea states.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results show that at least one period of roll motion can be accurately predicted in sea state 7 irregular long-crested waves.
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same predictive capability but specifies sea state 7 and irregular long-crested waves, narrowing and clarifying the original broader 'high sea states' claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The hybrid neural network, which combines LSTM and CNN, outperforms single LSTM and CNN methods in predicting the amplitude of roll angles.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed hybrid neural network combining LSTM and CNN demonstrates better performance in predicting the amplitude of roll angles compared to single LSTM and CNN methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same comparative performance claim for the hybrid LSTM+CNN model with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Selecting motion states and wave heights as feature space improves the prediction accuracy of ship roll motions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Selecting motion states and wave heights as feature space improves the prediction accuracy of ship roll motions.
alignments.rationale: The feature-selection conclusion is preserved verbatim in the publication claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel data-driven methodology for multi-step prediction of ship roll motions in high sea states is presented.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit high-level contribution statement about the methodology; while implied by the preprint results, this specific contribution phrasing was not listed among the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method demonstrates superior performance in 3D object detection by utilizing temporal information from multiple frames.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method detects 3D objects by exploiting temporal information in multiple frames, specifically point cloud videos.
alignments.rationale: The publication claim restates the same core contribution of exploiting temporal information in point-cloud videos for 3D detection.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Grid Message Passing Network (GMPNet) effectively encodes short-term data by mining motion cues from nearby frames.
alignments.pub_claim_text: GMPNet effectively encodes short-term data by constructing a k-NN graph with neighbor grids to mine motion cues.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds implementation detail (k-NN graph with neighbor grids) that clarifies how GMPNet encodes short-term motion cues without changing the claim's intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Attentive Spatiotemporal Transformer GRU (AST-GRU) enhances the ability to focus on small objects and align moving objects in long-term frames.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AST-GRU enhances the vanilla GRU with Spatial and Temporal Transformer Attention modules to better align moving objects and focus on small objects.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides more detail about AST-GRU's added attention modules, clarifying the mechanism while preserving the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The overall framework supports both online and offline video object detection in point clouds.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The overall framework supports both online and offline video object detection in point clouds.
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the same conclusion about supporting both online and offline video object detection.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Evaluation results on the nuScenes benchmark demonstrate that our method achieves superior performance, ranking first on the leaderboard at the time of paper submission.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds an explicit empirical performance claim about leaderboard ranking that was not present among the listed preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed novel conformal loss function approximates the traditionally two-step Conformal Prediction approach in a single step.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed novel conformal loss function approximates the traditionally two-step Conformal Prediction approach in a single step.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this core methodological contribution verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel loss function allows a Deep Learning model to learn the direct relationship between input data and conformal p-values.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not explicitly assert that the model learns a direct mapping to conformal p-values; that specific learning claim is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach achieves significant training time reductions up to 86% compared to Aggregated Conformal Prediction while maintaining comparable approximate validity and predictive efficiency.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach achieves significant training time reductions up to 86% compared to Aggregated Conformal Prediction.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly states the training-time reduction; it separates the claims about reductions and about maintaining validity/efficiency across two publication claims (the latter appears as a separate claim).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Self-consistent dynamical mean field theory (DMFT) can calculate cross covariances in random neural networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Self-consistent dynamical mean field theory (DMFT) can calculate cross covariances in random neural networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this methodological result identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Random neural networks can generate chaotic activity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study shows that random neural networks can generate chaotic activity.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same conclusion about chaotic activity generation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effective dimension of activity in a classic random-network model is extensive but fractionally low.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The effective dimension of activity in the random-network model is extensive but fractionally low.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this quantitative property of effective dimension without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed formulae for cross covariances generalize to non-i.i.d. couplings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The derived formulas for cross covariances generalize to non-i.i.d. couplings.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the generalization claim for the derived formulas with equivalent meaning and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed machine-learning approach can efficiently identify potentially exfoliable materials from bulk three-dimensional structures. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed machine-learning approach can efficiently identify potentially exfoliable materials from bulk three-dimensional structures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Wording and meaning are effectively identical between preprint and publication; only the claim type label changed from contribution to result, which does not alter the substantive claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The final machine-learning model achieves a very high recall of 98%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The final machine-learning model achieves a very high recall of 98%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Exact same numeric performance claim in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An online tool has been published that allows users to assess whether any 3D compound can be exfoliated into 2D layers. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An online tool has been published that allows users to assess whether any 3D compound can be exfoliated into 2D layers. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Identical contribution claim about the online tool in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study provides an intuitive explanation of the five most important variables of the machine-learning model using SHAP analysis. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study provides an intuitive explanation of the five most important variables of the machine-learning model using SHapely Additive exPlanations analysis. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same explanatory claim; publication expands the acronym SHAP to its full name but does not change substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed programming language is simple, intrinsically parallel, lock-free, and formally correct. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed programming language is simple, intrinsically parallel, lock-free, and formally correct. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Claim identical in both preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Turbo, the constraint solver developed, is entirely programmed on GPUs. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Turbo, the constraint solver developed, is entirely programmed on GPUs. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Directly matching statement about Turbo's GPU implementation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Turbo validates the correctness of the proposed approach. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Turbo validates the correctness of the proposed approach. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same validation claim present in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Turbo compares positively to a parallel CPU-based solver. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Turbo compares positively to a parallel CPU-based solver. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Performance-comparison claim unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new stroke rendering method is proposed that considers the tonal characteristics and representative color of the original oil painting. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new stroke rendering method is proposed that considers the tonal characteristics and representative color of the original oil painting. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The core contribution statement is the same in both documents.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method effectively fits the tone of the original oil painting image into the stylized image. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method can fit the tone of the original oil painting image into a stylized image while preserving the artist’s creative effect. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication adds the preservation of the artist’s creative effect, clarifying the qualitative outcome without changing the overall scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments have validated the efficacy of the proposed model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments have validated the efficacy of the proposed model in comparison to three state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication specifies that validation included comparisons to three state-of-the-art methods, a clarification that adds methodological detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method is more suitable for the works of pointillism painters with a relatively uniform sense of direction, especially for natural scenes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method is more suitable for the works of pointillism painters with a relatively uniform style, especially for natural scenes. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: Same limitation/conclusion about suitability for pointillism-style works; wording changed slightly but meaning preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: When the original painting brush strokes have a clearer sense of direction, the method may be less satisfactory in simulating brushwork texture features. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: This specific limitation about clearer stroke direction is not present in the publication's listed claims, indicating it was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work proposes a taxonomy for diagnosis computation methods. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose a taxonomy that allows for the standardized assessment, classification, and comparison of diagnosis algorithms. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert the proposal of a taxonomy; the publication phrasing emphasizes its assessment/classification functionality but does not change the core contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The taxonomy allows for standardized assessment, classification, and comparison of diagnostic techniques. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose a taxonomy that allows for the standardized assessment, classification, and comparison of diagnosis algorithms. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The functional claim about standardizing assessment/classification/comparison appears verbatim in the publication; classification as contribution vs result differs in label only.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed taxonomy facilitates the selection of the appropriate algorithm for specific diagnostic problems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The taxonomy facilitates the selection of the 'right' algorithm for specific problem cases in practical diagnostic settings. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication adds that the facilitation applies to practical diagnostic settings, narrowing the scope to practical cases while preserving the original conclusion.

alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate the value and application of the taxonomy by assessing and categorizing more than 30 important diagnostic methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This demonstrative result (assessment of >30 methods) appears in the publication but is not present in the preprint's listed claims, so it is an added publication claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Using the taxonomy as a common guideline for algorithm analysis would benefit the research community in various regards. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication includes an additional forward-looking claim about benefits to the community that was not in the preprint claims, thus treated as added.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best DNN model achieves an accuracy of 99.93% on the ASL Alphabet dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best DNN model achieves an accuracy of 99.93% on the ASL alphabet dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Exact numeric performance claim carried over unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best performing SNN model has an accuracy of 99.30% on the ASL Alphabet dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best performing SNN model has an accuracy of 99.30% on the ASL alphabet dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Performance claim identical between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Loihi neuromorphic hardware implementations achieve up to 20.64x reduction in power consumption compared to NCS2. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Loihi neuromorphic hardware implementations achieve up to 20.64× reduction in power consumption compared to NCS2. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same quantified hardware efficiency claim in both preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The best DNN model achieves an accuracy of 99.76% on the ASL-Digits dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best DNN model achieves an accuracy of 99.76% on the ASL-digits dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical accuracy claim for ASL-digits.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The SNN achieves 99.03% accuracy on the ASL-Digits dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: This explicit claim about recognition in uncontrolled environments appears in the publication but was not stated as a separate claim in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new feature vector computed from the observed time series is linearly separable, allowing for effective classification of connected and disconnected pairs of nodes.

alignments.rationale: The publication frames the same separability result more generally as enabling classifiers; the core technical claim (linear separability) is retained but presented more cleanly.

alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance claim about CNNs and sample complexity with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The trained CNNs generalize well over structurally distinct networks and noise-level profiles, as well as to real-world networks when trained on synthetic networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The trained CNNs generalize well over structurally distinct networks and noise-level profiles.
alignments.rationale: The publication separates generalization claims and downgrades certainty: it keeps strong claims about structural and noise robustness but treats real-world generalization as a distinct (lower-certainty) claim, narrowing and tempering the original combined assertion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method consistently reconstructs the graph in a pairwise manner, determining the presence or absence of edges between node pairs from their corresponding time series.
alignments.rationale: No explicit publication claim asserts the stronger statement that the method consistently reconstructs full graphs pairwise; related claims focus on per-pair features and classifiers but do not make the same reconstruction claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed feature-based paradigm allows for the computation of a feature vector from observed time series for each pair of nodes.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly frames the feature-computation paradigm as a contribution; while related to preprint content, this precise presentation as a standalone contribution was not separately stated in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The CNNs also generalize well to real-world networks while trained over a synthetic network.
alignments.rationale: The publication makes a distinct claim about real-world generalization (separated and given medium certainty) that was not presented as a separate confirmed result in the preprint.
